pattern: XOR a number with itself cancels to zero, so XOR-ing every element together leaves only the numbers that appeared an odd number of times; here duplicates appear twice (even), singletons once (odd), so a second pass XOR of elements seen before isolates the duplicates.
hints: XOR of a number with itself is 0, and XOR with 0 is the identity. How does parity of occurrences affect the running XOR?
Track which numbers you have already seen. When you encounter a number the second time, include it in the answer.
Maintain a set of seen values; for each element, if it is already in the set XOR it into the accumulator, otherwise add it to the set.

edge_inputs.description: minimum length single element no duplicate
maximum value elements both appearing twice
XOR of two equal duplicates is zero

stress.description: random mix of singletons and pairs with values 1-50
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    pool = list(range(1, 51))
    rng.shuffle(pool)
    n = min(size, 25)
    singles = pool[:n//2]
    doubles = pool[n//2:n]
    nums = singles + doubles + doubles
    rng.shuffle(nums)
    return [nums]
